players.name: Fernando Mendoza
strengths: Presence and decision making in the pocket
Accuracy and power in the intermediate
weaknesses: Lack of mobility against NFL athletes
players.outlook: The Raiders decided to add Kirk Cousins to give Mendoza time to transition to the NFL, and ensure their #1 pick can be successful. My pre-draft comp for Mendoza was Cousins, so this makes sense. A top-2 pick in dynasty superflex leagues still, Mendoza may not make an impact in redraft leagues this fall and Cousins should be considered the starter until further notice.

players.name: TY Simpson
players.outlook: Simpson landed in a great spot, but we won't see that for 2-3 years. Simpson will be the QB2 in LA until Stafford retires, but Stafford has also dealt with plenty of injury issues during his career. Simpson is a top-5 dynasty superflex pick, but would only be a redraft waiver add if Stafford is injured this year.

players.name: Carson Beck
players.outlook: The Cardinals are looking for their QB of the future after Jacoby Brissett bridges the gap. The Cardinals also have Gardner Minshew, so expect little time from Beck under center in 2026. This pick is for 2027 and beyond. Beck has a lot of work to do to become successful in the NFL, but he will get that work this year with an eye toward 2027 as the starter.

players.name: Drew Allar
strengths: Arm strength to make throws at all levels
weaknesses: Has issues placing ball where receiver has best chance to win
First look QB — issues adjusting mid-play
players.outlook: Allar was considered a top QB prospect prior to the season, but reports say Penn State let Allar down with his development. Maybe the Steelers will find gold here. Allar has all the tools to be successful in the NFL if he can clean up his accuracy and work on processing plays post-snap. I consider Allar to be QB3 behind Mendoza and Simpson with this landing spot.

players.name: Cade Klubnik
strengths: Solid accuracy when in a clean pocket
weaknesses: Needs to engage lower half more on harder throws
Progressions and extending plays disrupt throws
players.outlook: The Jets need a QB of the future and maybe Klubnik can offer some value. He has a lot to work on behind Geno Smith, but will need more than a year to develop. The speed of good college defenses gave him issues, and that won't get easier in the NFL. Klubnik looks like a career backup at best, but gives the Jets a project until they draft a QB in 2027.

players.name: Cole Payton
players.outlook: Payton could offer the Eagles a backup option behind Hurts in a year or two, but he is a very raw prospect with a lot of work ahead. It's more likely he was drafted for his athletic ability running the football and the Eagles will look to utilize that with specific packages to keep Hurts from taking so many hits.

players.name: Taylen Green
strengths: Works through progressions well in a clean pocket
players.outlook: Green is Anthony Richardson 2.0 with less hype and less arm talent. Green looks to make moves with his legs first and arm second. When the pocket closes, he would rather move and try to improvise. While his scrambling is elite, he has trouble throwing on the run and reading the defense outside the pocket. He could compete for QB2 in a couple years, but he is firmly ahead of Dillon Gabriel already.

players.name: Athan Kaliakmanis
strengths: Has improved each of the last three seasons
weaknesses: Needs play-action to help open the pass game
players.outlook: Kaliakmanis was not considered a draftable prospect by many, and seemed to be in line for a UDFA contract or a trip to a developmental league. He has some strengths that NFL teams feel they can convert into a backup role or emergency starter. With Jayden Daniels in Washington, it probably doesn't happen here.

players.name: Behren Morton
strengths: Will stay in the pocket till the last minute to complete progressions
Leads pass catchers well to keep them from big hits
players.outlook: Morton doesn't have much tape for evaluation due to injury history in college. He's tough and will play until he literally can't, but it has affected his development. He has good intangibles and with some healthy seasons, could grow into a backup role.

players.name: Garrett Nussmeier
strengths: Arm talent to drive ball down field and throw into tight windows
weaknesses: Needs to learn the touch pass
players.outlook: Nussmeier likely will not be taking many snaps in a Chiefs uniform. If he can be a competent backup in KC, the Chiefs will move him to a QB-needy team. Nussmeier could have been a first round pick had he come out a year early, so the fall to the 7th is interesting. From a dynasty perspective, investing in Nussmeier over several QBs before him makes more sense.

players.name: Jeremiyah Love
weaknesses: Lean frame could keep him from a three-down role
players.outlook: The Cardinals made this pick to sell tickets, and it's a tough landing spot for fantasy. Hopefully James Conner is released before training camp and Trey Benson is traded. In redraft leagues, Love has a RB2 ceiling knowing that Allgeier will still get touches and TDs. In dynasty, he is still a top-12 RB, but the Cardinals need to improve the O-line for that to happen.

players.name: Jadarian Price
strengths: Smooth hips and footwork to make defenders miss
Vision behind the LOS and in the open field
weaknesses: Lack of elite speed and breakaway play ability
players.outlook: Price decided to remain at Notre Dame and play alongside Heisman finalist Jeremiyah Love, but he could have been a top back at most other schools. Lacks elite speed and acceleration to be a home run threat and probably won't help in the pass game, but could be vital to any team as a two-down back with a nose for extra yards.

players.name: Kaelon Black
players.outlook: The 49ers want to limit McCaffrey's touches, and Black is the exact opposite of CMC. A hard runner who will pick up yards, but lacks creativity. If his lane is closed, he will still run there. Not great out of cuts and lacks burst. Will succeed only as a goal-line and short yardage back.

players.name: Jonah Coleman
weaknesses: Doesn't run hard into or through contact
players.outlook: Coleman will likely find himself behind Harvey and Dobbins in 2026 with an eye toward a pass catching role in 2027. Does not profile as a three-down back, but will be good on third downs due to pass blocking and catching ability. Coleman will likely be the #2 to Harvey in 2027 with a change of pace role at best. Not a strong enough runner to see double digit touches a game.

players.name: Mike Washington
weaknesses: Slow to hit openings in the O-line
players.outlook: Washington will be the perfect complement to Jeanty in LV. Klint Kubiak frustrated fantasy owners with the Walker/Charbonnet split in Seattle, and now he could do the same thing in LV. Washington has more big play ability than Jeanty and owners should handcuff him in dynasty. In redraft, he probably is only a waiver add if Jeanty gets hurt, but could sneak into the flex if the offense takes a big step forward.

players.name: Emmett Johnson
strengths: Stays at top speed through breaks and cuts
weaknesses: Lack of vision behind the LOS
Not creative in the run game — will stick to the call
players.outlook: Johnson will be a nice complement to Kenneth Walker, and given Walker's injury history, Johnson could be a priority waiver add in 2026 redraft leagues. Johnson is a better all-around back than Brashard Smith and could take the RB2 role right away. In dynasty, adding Johnson should be a priority for Walker owners as insurance in the event Walker misses time.

players.name: Nicholas Singleton
strengths: Pass catching and special teams ability
weaknesses: Always runs at top speed, which affects his ability to visualize lanes and make defenders miss
players.outlook: Singleton to Tennessee is one of the better fantasy fits in this class. Tony Pollard may only have one season left in Tennessee and Tyjae Spears' knee has always been a concern. Singleton could start to carve out a role in 2026 if Pollard doesn't start well, and should be the RB1 for the Titans in 2027. Should be one of the first RBs drafted in dynasty rookie drafts.

players.name: Adam Randall
strengths: Sharp cuts and acceleration out of them
Route tree as a former WR
weaknesses: Had trouble finding the open lane behind the LOS
players.outlook: A former WR with only one season at RB. A perfect project for the Ravens to pair with Derrick Henry for the next couple of seasons. Could take over for Justice Hill immediately due to his pass catching ability. Progressed well as a runner last season, and could be a solid fantasy contributor in a couple years after he grows into the role. Worth an add in the second round of rookie drafts.

players.name: Kaytron Allen
strengths: Built like and hits like a truck
Vision and patience allows blocks and openings to develop
players.outlook: Jacory Croskey-Merritt was inconsistent last year and Rachaad White is on a one-year contract. Allen could push JCM for his role in 2026 if JCM can't find more consistency. If Allen shows enough this season, he could earn the RB1 role in 2027. Long-term opportunity is there for dynasty rookie draft adds, while redraft leagues should just add him to the watch list.

players.name: Demond Claiborne
strengths: Patience to find open holes and create plays out of nothing
Elite acceleration out of cuts and stops
weaknesses: Will run past blocking before it is set
players.outlook: Claiborne could use a year behind Aaron Jones and Jordan Mason, then he'll turn into the perfect complement to Mason. Claiborne can turn nothing into something and if the Vikings pass game takes a big step forward, he could be a home run threat in 2027, even if it does come with inconsistencies. Probably no fantasy value in 2026 for redraft unless the Vikings lose multiple RBs.

players.name: Eli Heidenreich
strengths: Great at the point of catch
weaknesses: Lacks acceleration and top-end speed to be an elite NFL weapon
players.outlook: Dreaming up plays for Heidenreich will probably be more fun than his actual production. He seems headed for a special teams role instead of being utilized as a weapon on offense. The Steelers could find a way to use him from the slot from time to time, but his short stature doesn't make him a great target. Any fantasy potential will likely never be reached.

players.name: Seth McGowan
strengths: Lets blocks develop in front of him
weaknesses: Focus issues cause drops in the pass game
players.outlook: McGowan likely slips into an RB3 role right away and could be used in pass blocking situations this season. Doesn't offer much upside as a runner, with only 10 carries of 10+ yards last season and a longest run of 20 yards. Don't expect McGowan to have any fantasy value in 2026 or beyond.

players.name: Jam Miller
weaknesses: Lacks vision and discipline behind the LOS
Doesn't make tacklers miss in open space
players.outlook: Miller is not a good blocker or pass catcher, so he is already limited in what he can do to find the field. He doesn't create many yards in the run game, but has good enough size and lower half to pile through defenders for short yardage. Lack of burst and athleticism could also keep him off special teams. His route to any time on an NFL field seems limited.

players.name: Jmari Taylor
strengths: Can run routes from the slot or out wide
weaknesses: Will take too much contact instead of creating opportunity
Slow out of cuts and stops
players.outlook: Taylor did not hear his name called in the draft, but went to Jacksonville as a UDFA. Bhayshul Tuten will get his shot as RB1 in 2026 and the Jags added Chris Rodriguez as well. Taylor will be more of a pass catching/change of pace back with an eye toward a third-down role in the future.

players.name: Leveon Moss
strengths: Finishes runs through contact and maintains balance for extra yards
weaknesses: Straight runner with underwhelming cut ability
players.outlook: Moss would be a perfect complement to De'Von Achane due to his short yardage and goal line ability. But there are several backs in front of him to find chances. Ollie Gordon already fits the same role, while Jaylen Wright and Donovan Edwards are suitable backups with more explosive ability.

players.name: Carnell Tate
strengths: Expansive route tree and runs them all well
Works back to his QB when in trouble
weaknesses: Will make big plays, but not typically after the catch
players.outlook: Cam Ward gets his WR1 and Tate gets an opportunity to be the playmaker in an ascending offense. Ward improved each week last season and if he takes a big step, Tate would easily be top 24. Its still a what if and Wandale Robinson will get plenty of targets as well. In year one, Tate could be closer to Jameson Williams production with big weeks carrying his overall season numbers.

players.name: Jordyn Tyson
weaknesses: Body catches too often in traffic
players.outlook: As long as Tyson stays healthy, Tyler Shough is good enough to make him and Olave both top 24 WRs. Tyson has more upside TD potential on a weekly basis playing on the outside, while the volume will likely benefit Olave more every week. Biggest issue for Tyson during his career will be staying healthy, and the one reason why other WRs may be a safer bet in dynasty formats for early rookie picks.

players.name: Makai Lemon
strengths: Tough at the point of catch
weaknesses: Dosent always allow route to develop
players.outlook: Lemon could be the best and most consistent WR from the 2026 class, but his landing spot (for now) is not great. AJ Brown is still likely to be traded, but this offense is prone to inconsistencies and already has a WR1 in DeVonta Smith. Dallas Goedert is back for another season and Philly drafted Eli Stowers. Lemon is behind Tate and Tyson for year one production, but long term value is still very much in play.

players.name: KC Concepcion
strengths: Acceleration at LOS and out of his breaks
players.outlook: We dont know who the QB will be in 2026, but CLE all of the sudden has a ton of playmakers. Concepcion and Boston were drafted this year, they still have Jeudy and Tillman, plus Harold Fannin at TE. We will likely need to be cautious in 2026 until the Browns have their QB and we need to wait for some WRs to leave Cleveland. Concepcion has a chance to become a premier slot playmaker in dynasty leagues though.

players.name: Omar Cooper Jr
strengths: Plays physical with and without the ball
weaknesses: Slow and stiff in and out of breaks
Does not adjust well mid play
players.outlook: The Jets have playmakers, but they all resemble each other. Wilson, Cooper and Sadiq all play a similar role, so how will the Jets utilize them? That answer could kill Coopers value. Redraft value is not great right now, but investing in dynasty is the wise choice still. Also, Geno Smith is not the answer at QB, so Coopers long term value is tied to a lot of what ifs.

players.name: Dezhaun Stribling
strengths: Uses arm length to make catches over DBs
weaknesses: Could be stronger at the point of catch
Tough DBs cause issues in traffic
players.outlook: Stribling fits the 49ers well with superior run blocking, but you dont get fantasy points for that. Stribling will need to add strength before he becomes a consistent fantasy producer, but with Evans aging and Pearsall injury concerns, Stribling could see action in 2026. To start, hes the WR4 in SF, but could be WR2 as early as 2027.

players.name: Denzel Boston
players.outlook: The Browns wanted to add weapons and they added two of the better WRs in this draft class. Boston is likely behind Concepcion for potential work in 2026 though. Boston will play a role similar to what Jeudy or Tillman will, so one of or both will need to be gone before Boston finds his ceiling. Since Boston will operate outside, away from Concepcion and Fannin, his upside long term may be higher than both.

players.name: Germie Bernard
strengths: Route tree from outside or slot
Can tempo his speeds to what fits route best
players.outlook: Bernard can be a versatile weapon for the Steelers. Likely to see the slot to begin with Metcalf and Pittman outside, targets will be tough to come by early in his career. Will Bernard, Washington or Freiermuth control the middle of the field? Whoever is the QB in 2026 for PIT will have plenty of options, but Bernard is likely headed towards 3rd most targets on this team at best, 5th at worst.

players.name: Antonio Williams
strengths: Hard to catch in the middle of the field
weaknesses: Needs to be stronger against press coverage
players.outlook: Williams is tough to catch after the catch. Not super fast but very hard to wrap up and bring down. Working out of the slot in Washington will be a great fit with Terry McLaurin outside. Luke McCaffrey will start the year ahead of Williams, but Williams can take it from him this year. Williams will be a better version of what Washington wanted to do with Deebo last year.

players.name: Malachi Fields
weaknesses: Does not work back to QB well on broken plays
players.outlook: Fields will fit great in New York opposite of Nabers. Fields has elite downfield ability to high point catches and beat defenders mid-air. Not fast enough to beat CB1, but with Nabers he will not have to worry about that coverage. Can make a difference in 2026 but with inconsistencies. Has the making of a solid big play WR2 moving forward.

players.name: Caleb Douglas
weaknesses: Dosent sit and work out of breaks quick enough
players.outlook: Miami reached for Douglas as he will be more of a project than Chris Bell who Miami took at the end of the 3rd. Douglas is tall with a large wingspan that allows him to make insane catches. His route running needs a lot of work and he will need to add strength for a better contested catch ability. Douglas is probably closer to just a depth piece for Miami than actual fantasy contributor.

players.name: Zachariah Branch
strengths: Can turn short gains into huge plays
Accelerates fast out of cuts and breaks
weaknesses: Route running needs to be refined
Utilization at Georgia does not translate to NFL well
players.outlook: Branch was not set up for success in the NFL by Georgia, which is odd to say. He was utilized more on passes behind the LOS, screens and was not asked to run much of a route tree. But Branch is fast and explosive. He can take any play to the house or make huge gains. He may end up being one of the steals in the draft in 2-3 years. Dont let your leaguemates stash him in dynasty.

players.name: Jakobi Lane
weaknesses: Can be jammed too often at the line
Struggles to change speeds to routes
Struggles with contact early in route
players.outlook: Lane is a great red zone threat due to length and catch radius. The physicality of NFL CBs will throw Lane off his routes early and cause delays for his QB. Putting on weight and strength will be important. The Ravens also added Elijah Sarratt in the fourth round. Either Lane or Sarratt will likely start opposite of Rashod Bateman in 2026, but Lane is the closest to fantasy relevancy, although 2027 may be the earliest he helps.

players.name: Chris Brazzell II
weaknesses: Ran limited intermediate routes in college
Has a tough time with physicality in middle of field
players.outlook: Brazzell finds himself in a great spot to begin producing right away. Brazzell will fight with Legette to start opposite Tet McMillan. Brazzell is the field stretcher to take this Carolina offense to the next level and while many are sold on Coker as a breakout candidate, Brazzell is more talented and fits as the WR2 better in this offense. 2026 could start slow, but Brazzell could be making big plays by the end of the year.

players.name: Ted Hurst
weaknesses: Can be more physical on 50/50 balls
players.outlook: The Bucs have plenty of pass catchers in 2026. While Hurst has the ability to succeed at the NFL level, it will take time to clear a path to targets. Hurst will begin his career as the field stretcher this offense needs, leading to some big plays but inconsistent targets. As he transitions to tougher competition, Hurst could find himself as a playmaker all over the field.

players.name: Zavion Thomas
strengths: Vision and burst with ball in hand
weaknesses: May profile more as a RB in the NFL
Route running is well below NFL standard
players.outlook: The move to RB may be needed for Thomas in the NFL. Hes not a great route runner, struggles to consistently catch the ball and was probably drafted as a return specialist more than anything. Thomas will be a project for Ben Johnson that may return value in the next few years, but as of now, he has little to no fantasy value.

players.name: Chris Bell
players.outlook: Bell is going to be the big, physical threat for the Dolphins once he returns fully from his ACL tear. Bell lacks high end athletic traits, but can be very physical on the outside and down the field. Hard to expect any type of consistent production in 2026, but Bell could be WR1 in year two. For him to be successful though, he needs a threat on his opposite side as well.

players.name: Brenen Thompson
players.outlook: This is an easy evaluation. The fastest player in this class, Thompson is a field stretcher. His value is pulling defenders deep, not in his ability to actually catch the ball. Thompson will have some big plays from time to time, but lacks a lot of qualities to become a top option consistently.

players.name: Elijah Sarratt
weaknesses: Loses speed during more complicated routes
players.outlook: Sarratt always finds a way to get open, but he is average at a lot of things without excelling at enough. Will become a solid red zone weapon, or a physical slot receiver, Sarratt will be athletically limited at the NFL level. Lane and Sarratt both will take time to adjust to the NFL game, but Lane is more athletically gifted and will likely be more fantasy relevant over Sarratt during their time in the NFL.

players.name: Kaden Wetjen
weaknesses: Tries for big plays too often
players.outlook: Wetjen will almost likely be a return specialist at the NFL level. While he could see some plays from the slot from time to time, he will make and maintain an NFL roster spot based on his ability to flip the field on punt returns. Should only be considered in dynasty leagues with dedicated points to return yards.

players.name: Skyler Bell
weaknesses: Ability to run full route tree
players.outlook: Bell having time to learn from DJ Moore will be great for his development. Bell needs to learn the full route tree and grow from being a field stretcher to an all around WR. However, his ability to make plays after the catch could make him an interesting option in this Bears offense in 2-3 years. Taking a shot late on Bell in dynasty leagues could prove fruitful long term.

players.name: Bryce Lance
weaknesses: Big numbers against low level competition
Route running and agility in middle of field
players.outlook: Bryce Lance has gaudy numbers the last two years, but hasnt faced very tough competition at NDSU. Will play well on the outside and is very athletic at the point of catch, along with great ball tracking abilities. But his athletic traits dont translate well to the intermediate part of the field. Goes to a great spot to get on the field early, but more of a long term stash in dynasty leagues.

players.name: Colbie Young
weaknesses: Does not separate well at the LOS or down the field
players.outlook: This could have been a huge reach by the Bengals given Youngs off field issues and injury issues. Young has the size and strength you want from an NFL WR, and plays like it. He lacks the ability to create separation, mostly from speed and acceleration. Young could make his mark on NFL fields as a red zone threat a couple of years down the road.

players.name: Reggie Virgil
strengths: Works back to QB when play breaks down
weaknesses: Tends to work too quickly through route, instead of letting it develop
players.outlook: Virgil took a large leap from the MAC to the Big 12 and performed well. He has solid footwork and attention to detail, works hard to refine his craft. He plays too fast on the field sometimes and dosent always allow the route to develop. Working alongside Harrison Jr, Wilson and McBride will help him potentially develop into a WR3 option in ARI.

players.name: Kendrick Law
strengths: Could be a solid kick returner
weaknesses: Was used as a gadget player more than true WR
players.outlook: The Lions have likely invested in Law as a return specialist. He was solid in special teams at Alabama, but once he got to Kentucky, he was used more for plays at or behind the line of scrimmage. Law is a WR4 at best in Detroit.

players.name: Cyrus Allen
strengths: CBs have a tough time sticking to him
weaknesses: Didnt see much pressure on LOS in college
Routes need to be more sharp and crisp
players.outlook: Allen will likely not have the size/strength needed to play outside in the NFL, but could be a menace over the middle once Travis Kelce retires. Its hard to keep up with Allen due to his blend of speed and burst. If Rice continues to have off-field issues, Allen would fit in perfectly for him in the slot. In two years, Rice could go outside with Worthy and Allen takes over in the slot.

players.name: Kevin Coleman Jr
strengths: Hard to wrap up cleanly after the catch
weaknesses: Dosent work back to his QB
players.outlook: The third of three WRs drafted by the Dolphins this year. Coleman is a project that needs to add strength, clean up his footwork and learn a larger route tree. He has good hands and is really hard to bring down post catch. Coleman will likely be a depth piece for the Dolphins more than a consistent contributor.

players.name: Barion Brown
weaknesses: Lacks basic WR fundamentals including route tree, ball tracking and body control
players.outlook: Brown was drafted for his special teams ability. He is a gifted kick returner that can make an impact at the NFL level, but he is too raw of a prospect to make an impact at WR.

players.name: Josh Cameron
strengths: Tough to cover in the end zone
weaknesses: Lacks burst and top end speed to be a big playmaker
players.outlook: Cameron may project more as a receiving tight end than a wide receiver. He is not very quick or fast, but is a menace to cover in the end zone. A transition to a new position may be best for him to stick in the NFL.

players.name: Malik Benson
strengths: Is able to accelerate past press coverage with ease
weaknesses: Needs to add more route tree knowledge
players.outlook: Benson will stick in the NFL due to his big play ability. In a couple of years he could be the type of deep threat that shows up a few times a year with a big TD, but he wont be a volume monster. Keep his name in mind for best ball leagues after he adapts to the NFL game.

players.name: CJ Daniels
strengths: Great knack for getting to the ball before defender
weaknesses: Will have troubles with stronger CBs
players.outlook: Daniels will be a nice dart throw in a couple of years after he has time to add some strength. Biggest issue will be staying healthy. Daniels will be the deep threat the Rams need after Adams retires, but it will take time for him to actually materialize into a consistent performer.

players.name: Emmanuel Henderson Jr
strengths: Special teams ability both returning and defending
players.outlook: Henderson is the classic hard worker pick. He will likely only be a special teams player in the NFL, but could be a very good one. He is a former running back turned WR that did not produce much in college outside special teams.

players.name: CJ Williams
weaknesses: Did not showcase elite metrics on film or testing
players.outlook: Williams joins a Jags team loaded at WR now with little room to find his way to the field soon. Williams had very little college production prior to 2025 when he transferred to Stanford. With very little production or testing metrics that showcased elite talents, Williams will likely land on a practice squad.

players.name: Lewis Bond
strengths: Leadership ability and locker room guy
players.outlook: Bond will be a locker room guy that coaches know will follow directions and work hard. Hes not very fast and lacks burst at the LOS and coming out of breaks in his routes. He is also not very strong in his routes or at the point of catch.

players.name: Anthony Smith
weaknesses: A very lanky player that needs to add muscle
players.outlook: Smith was known for his big plays at East Carolina and we could see that from time to time in the NFL. He will be given a chance to utilize his speed on special teams to start while adding the strength needed to play WR at the NFL level.

players.name: Deion Burks
weaknesses: Needs to clean up footwork on routes
players.outlook: Burks had a fantastic combine and was considered a mid round pick at worst, so falling to the end of the 7th round was surprising. Burks has elite playmaking ability but landed in a great spot. If Alec Pierce deals with injuries or missed time, Burks has the big play ability to stand in easily.

players.name: Kenyon Sadiq
players.outlook: Sadiq is unlike athletes we have seen at TE before. He truly could move to WR and make plays right away. While he needs to refine his game and not rely on pure athleticism, his playmaking ability is unmatched by most other players in this draft. Take your time with Sadiq and he will have upside.

players.name: Eli Stowers
weaknesses: Needs to clean up route running
Tries to let the ball come to him, instead of fighting for it
players.outlook: Stowers is a former QB, who only has 3 years at TE under his belt. Sitting behind Goedert for a year will do him wonders as he continues to learn how to play the position. In redraft, you wont see much production, but he could be a classic TE breakout in a year or two with PHI. Hes going to be a long term hold, so show patience.

players.name: Nate Boerkircher
players.outlook: This was a major reach for a team that already has a talented TE and tons of pass catching options. Boerkircher profiles more as a blocking TE in 12 personnel. Boerkircher has far too many inconsistencies as a pass catcher to make any fantasy impact soon.

players.name: Marlin Klein
strengths: Can gather himself after the catch for solid yards
players.outlook: Klein appears to be a backup TE that can make plays from time to time. From Germany, Klein hasnt been playing football for an extended period, so he has a lot of growth that is needed. If he can add some muscle and become a more consistent blocker, Klein could be a solid red zone target in a few years.

players.name: Max Klare
strengths: Solid route inventory and route running ability
players.outlook: Klare is highly athletic and could be an impact offensive TE down the road, but he dosent appear interested in blocking and has far too many drops that will lose his QBs trust at the NFL level. The Rams have several TE options already on the roster, so Klare appears headed for a backup role for a while. He has some of the best fantasy upside though of this years TE class.

players.name: Sam Roush
strengths: Best blocking TE in this class
weaknesses: Not an explosive athlete before or after the catch
Needs to utilize his strength at the catch as well
players.outlook: Another questionable TE pick early in the draft, but its clear Roush is headed to Chicago as a 12 personnel blocker. His size, strength and blocking ability will make him an extension of the o-line, but he lacks a lot of qualities as a pass catcher to ever be fantasy viable. His only fantasy contribution will be for the RBs he springs loose while blocking.

players.name: Oscar Delp
strengths: Beats LBs and slot CBs with ease
weaknesses: Tends to shy away from contact
players.outlook: Delp is a serious athlete before the catch, beating coverage with ease at times. He looks like a diet version of former UGA TE Brock Bowers. But after the catch, Delp seems to shy away from contact and does not fight for many yards. His catch radius is also very small, minimizing his upside. Delp is an athletic project that could help as a third option in the passing game for NO in a couple of years.

players.name: Will Kacmarek
players.outlook: Kacmarek is a boring TE, which isnt a bad thing, but not great for fantasy. He blocks well in the run game, but needs work on his technique for pass blocking. He will catch balls thrown to him, but wont make the adjustments on bad balls or gain yards after the catch.

players.name: Eli Raridon
strengths: Good speed to beat defenders on seam routes
weaknesses: Dosent attack the second level run blocking
players.outlook: Raridon is another athletic pass catching TE, but has a lot of growth. One of the better ceilings for this years class, he is very good with seam routes and beating smaller defenders for contested catches. He needs to add muscle to his tall frame and can improve as a blocker to stay on the field more. Raridon already has two torn ACLs in college though, so health will be key.

players.name: Matthew Hibner
strengths: Runs seam routes with the best TEs in college
weaknesses: Needs to be able to run more routes more efficiently
players.outlook: Hibner can run the seam with his speed alongside the best TEs in this class. Outside of that, he offers little upside to NFL rosters. In a few years, he could play a small Mark Andrews role but dosent offer the same upside or the same route tree. Hibner will be on the field to split the defense and drag safeties out of the box, not much more.

players.name: Justin Joly
strengths: Hard to contain in man to man coverage
weaknesses: Needs more effort in run blocking
players.outlook: Joly is very athletic and is hard to keep up with in man to man coverage. He needs more concentration pass catching to avoid drops or get to balls he could catch, but gives up on. Joly will be the perfect Sean Payton project over the next couple of years while he works on his technique. Joly could offer solid upside, but he has a while to get there.

players.name: Max Bredeson
weaknesses: Has only played fullback in college
players.outlook: Bredeson was drafted as a TE, but was the ultimate fullback at Michigan. He is underwhelming as an NFL prospect, but has the high motor to make up for it. Could help lead block as a fullback or be an extension of the o-line. He wont ever be a pass catcher though, even if he was drafted as a TE.

players.name: Tanner Koziol
strengths: Solid pass catching technique all around
weaknesses: Lacks size and strength to be a TE
players.outlook: Koziol will likely move to WR at some point as his size will never be impressive at the NFL level for a TE. He is a natural pass catcher though and could turn into a solid depth piece for the Jags down the road.

players.name: Riley Nowakowski
strengths: Natural run blocking technique and execution
weaknesses: Lacks athleticism as a pass catcher
players.outlook: Nowakowski could actually be a solid offensive lineman if he was taller and bigger due to natural blocking ability, but he will only be a TE2 in 12 personnel at best during his NFL career.

players.name: Joe Royer
strengths: Solid footwork as a pass catcher and route runner
weaknesses: Basic level of route tree knowledge
players.outlook: After little time on the field for three years at Ohio State, Royer transferred to Cincinnati. He has the ability and drive to improve in several areas of his game, but probably will never reach the level of being a consistent contributor on the field.

players.name: Josh Cuevas
weaknesses: Will likely be moved to fullback
players.outlook: Cuevas is undersized and underwhelming as a tight end, but his blocking ability will allow for a transition to fullback at some point. Since he has TE experience, he could still be used as a pass catcher out of the backfield at times as well.

players.name: Seydou Traore
strengths: Former soccer goalkeeper leads to good instincts and footwork
weaknesses: Not a natural pass catcher or football player
players.outlook: Expect Traore to find himself on the practice squad for an extended period while the Dolphins try to turn this former soccer goalkeeper into a tight end.

players.name: Bauer Sharp
strengths: Former QB with high football IQ
weaknesses: Still learning how to play TE
players.outlook: Sharp was a QB at Southeastern Louisiana, so he is sharp and has the football IQ. He then transferred to Oklahoma and then LSU while playing TE. Sharp is a TE project like a majority of the QBs in this league.

players.name: Jack Endries
strengths: Can stretch the field and beat man coverage
players.outlook: Endries was thought to be a round 4-5 TE but fell to Cincinnati in the 7th round. He is a natural pass catcher and was the leading receiver for Fernando Mendoza at Cal in 2024. Im very interested in his growth even after slipping so far in the draft.

players.name: Jaren Kanak
weaknesses: Multiple position flips since high school
players.outlook: Kanak was a mobile high school QB, then moved to linebacker in college before switching to TE for his final season. Kanak has not spent enough time at TE to make any type of impact, let alone be drafted as one. Hell stick on a practice squad, but could potentially move back to defense.

players.name: Carsen Ryan
players.outlook: Ryan is an underwhelming prospect that does the little things well but dosent do anything well enough to be a consistent playmaker at the NFL level.

players.name: Dallen Bentley
strengths: Blocks well at the second level
weaknesses: Only one year of solid college production
players.outlook: Bentley only had three catches during his first two years at Utah before 48 last season. Bentley can get to the second level to knock down linebackers, but dosent block well against defensive ends. Another project TE for the Broncos who will likely spend a majority of his time on a practice squad.
